pages.full_text:
__Tuesday 8th September__  Simon, Eve + Tinder returned to the farm. William's birthday + more hop-picking. We returned on Saturday 12th September.

__Sunday September 27th 2020__
Two days ago we got the keys to George Rd. At 9.15. we set off to John's with 2 large palm pot plants + other things that John wants from 86. Sheep on the road on the downhill road into Monmouth held us up for ages.
We met John, Kay + Eben at the 3 Horseshoes. Lovely sunshine. Pub lunch. Simon road Eben a book on Quantum Mechanics. Back to their house for a cup of tea + chat. Then home, via a pretty field with a view of Symonds Yat.
   Peter is taking the van with the American beds in down to London. In the end, he didn't have to - both beds fitted in his green Mercedes-
amazing!
__Sunday October 4th.__
At 2.30. we drove off to the farm with the white sofa + chair from the living room, 4 kitchen chairs + 4 Gordon Russell chairs. William + Bryony
pages.notes: Page explicitly states 'Sunday September 27th 2020', placing this entry in 2020 — beyond the stated volume range of 2003-2019. The volume may extend slightly past 2019. 'George Rd' likely refers to a property address. '86' likely refers to a previous house number address. 'the farm' and 'hop-picking' suggest a hop farm visit, possibly in Kent or Herefordshire. 'Gordon Russell chairs' refers to furniture by British designer Gordon Russell.

pages.full_text: had been to Broadstairs for 2 nights. It rained so Louise looked after Seb + Nina. Joseph boarded, but was sick, so they had to come back early. We chatted, but didn't go in because of Covid restrictions. Sunshine on the way home.
pages.notes: This page is a continuation of text from the left page (the Sunday October 4th entry continuing from the left page's final line). The ink here appears to be pencil/grey rather than blue as on the left page. The text refers to Covid restrictions, consistent with the 2020 date established on the left page. 'Broadstairs' is a seaside town in Kent. 'Joseph boarded' likely means the dog boarded at kennels.
